title: Synchronous git process spawning during array sorting (forensic scan)
description: In collectEntryPoints, the sort comparator calls readGitChurnWeight for compared elements, which runs execFileSync to spawn a synchronous git process. Sorting K elements is O(K log K) comparisons, so it can trigger hundreds of synchronous external process spawns, completely blocking the main event loop.
fix_direction: Pre-calculate git churn weights for all entry points once into a Map, then look them up O(1) in the comparator.

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Missing ARIA tree roles and keyboard focusability in registry tree nodes
description: Registry tree nodes (.fabric/rules folder, global.md, dashboard.md, ui-conventions.md) are interactive (hover styles, pointer cursor) but built as plain divs lacking tabindex=0, keyboard activation/navigation listeners, and semantic ARIA roles (role=tree/treeitem, aria-expanded). The entire left registry pane is inaccessible to keyboard/screen-reader users.
fix_direction: Wrap the tree container in role=tree, give each node role=treeitem + tabindex=0, and add keydown handlers for arrows and Enter/Space to manage focus/selection.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Dormant cross-store reference lint / personal-leak guard never invoked on write path
description: Cross-store reference linting (S49-lint) and personal-to-shared leak guards (isPersonalLeakIntoSharedStore) are fully defined and tested under packages/shared, but never imported/invoked in any production write path (extract-knowledge.ts, store-ops.ts, HTTP write APIs). So personal-layer (KP-*) knowledge/references can be written to shared stores without any real-time validation or blocking.
fix_direction: Import and execute lintCrossStoreReferences and isPersonalLeakIntoSharedStore inside knowledge write-path services (e.g. extractKnowledge) and block persistence on violation.

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Path traversal -> arbitrary file write via unsanitized project_id in bindings snapshot
description: bindingsSnapshotPath joins globalRoot with a dynamically loaded projectId from project config without sanitization. A cloned repo with a malicious project_id containing traversal ('../../../../.zshrc') will, on fabric store bind / sync / install, trigger writeBindingsSnapshot and overwrite arbitrary local files.
fix_direction: Sanitize projectId to strictly alphanumeric (regex/Zod in fabricConfigSchema) and verify the resolved snapshot path starts with the expected global bindings directory.

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Synchronous process spawning inside sort comparator during forensic entry-point ranking
description: The forensic scanner sorts all detected entry points with a comparator calling readGitChurnWeight per comparison, which synchronously spawns a child process (execFileSync git log) with no caching. O(N log N) comparisons cause hundreds of synchronous spawns (50 files -> 500+ spawns), freezing the CLI, starving the event loop, and risking FD exhaustion.
fix_direction: Pre-compute git churn weight per entry point once (or a lazy cache Map), then do fast in-memory numeric comparison during sort.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Ignored/discarded MCP tool arguments (layer_filter & target_paths)
description: The MCP tool definitions for fab_plan_context and fab_recall declare layer_filter (and target_paths for plan_context), but the registration callbacks in plan-context.ts and recall.ts omit these fields from destructuring and never pass them to the underlying services. Any client/LLM-supplied values are silently discarded.
fix_direction: Destructure layer_filter and target_paths in registerPlanContext and layer_filter in registerRecall, pass them to the services, and extend PlanContextInput to declare layer_filter.

context.notes: Discovered by security perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: YAML frontmatter injection via unescaped array joining (review merge/patch)
description: In rewriteFrontmatterMerge and appendPatchLines, array fields (tags, relevance_paths) are serialized into YAML frontmatter by joining elements with ', ' with no quoting/escaping. An element containing commas, brackets, newlines, or quotes corrupts the YAML flow-array syntax or allows arbitrary frontmatter injection.
fix_direction: Map each array item through an escaping/quoting helper (quoteIfNeeded / quoteRelevancePath) to escape backslashes, quotes, newlines, brackets before joining.

context.notes: Discovered by security perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: O(N * M) nested array intersection loop on recall path
description: On the recall path, candidate IDs and requested IDs are intersected with nested loops (effectiveIds.filter + requestedIds.includes), giving O(N*M) instead of O(N+M).
fix_direction: Convert candidateIds and requestedIds to Sets for O(1) membership, yielding O(N+M).

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Sequential N+1 file reads on the hot path of MCP tool calls
description: ensureKnowledgeFresh (called on every MCP tool invocation after a 500ms cooldown) loops every knowledge markdown file and sequentially awaits processSingleFile (readFile + sha256). For larger KBs this is hundreds of sequential blocking I/O ops on the hot path.
fix_direction: Parallelize with Promise.all and check mtimeMs from stat to skip reading/hashing unchanged files.

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Repeated loading/re-parsing of event ledger on every HTTP session call
description: JsonlEventStore.readEvents loads and parses the entire event ledger from disk on every lookup (get stream ID, replay events). As the ledger grows this causes linear degradation and repeated disk I/O on the streamable-HTTP hot path.
fix_direction: Add an in-memory cache of projected events in JsonlEventStore so the ledger file is not repeatedly read/parsed.

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Blocking synchronous recursive directory walk on Express event loop
description: GET /api/scan calls walkFiles, a fully blocking synchronous recursive directory walk (readdirSync/statSync) on the Express event loop thread. For repos with thousands of files this hangs the HTTP server for seconds.
fix_direction: Refactor walkFiles to async fs promises (readdir/stat) or delegate the scan to a worker thread.

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Unhandled git rebase error crashes sync continue/abort flow
description: runContinueSync and runAbortSync invoke defaultRebaseContinue / defaultRebaseAbort without catching thrown errors. If git rebase --continue/--abort fails (unstaged conflicts, other git errors), the exception propagates uncaught and crashes the CLI, leaving sync-session.json in a conflicted/stale state and locking the CLI in an unrecoverable sync loop.
fix_direction: Wrap the rebase ops in try-catch in runContinueSync/runAbortSync, record failure/abort cleanup in the state machine/session file, and return a clean error diagnostic instead of crashing.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Non-atomic session writes and unhandled parse error crash the CLI
description: saveSession writes sync-session.json with writeFileSync directly (no write-tmp+rename, no fsync); interruption/power loss corrupts it. loadSession parses via JSON.parse(readFileSync(...)) with no try-catch, so any later command (fabric sync --continue) crashes with an unhandled SyntaxError, permanently breaking sync until the session file is manually deleted.
fix_direction: Use atomicWriteJson (with fsync) in saveSession, and wrap JSON.parse in loadSession in try-catch to handle corruption gracefully (warn + return null / reclaim new session).

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: TOCTOU race and lock theft in HTTP server serve-lock
description: acquireLock verifies lock existence with fs.existsSync(p) then fs.writeFileSync(p,...), a TOCTOU race. Two concurrently starting HTTP servers can both find it empty/stale and write, one silently overwriting the other -> double execution. On shutdown releaseLock checks state.pid===process.pid and unlinks; if a new process overtook the lock, the old process's release deletes the active lock, causing accidental release.
fix_direction: Acquire atomically with fs.writeFileSync(p, content, { flag: 'wx' }) / openSync(p,'wx') so only one process succeeds; throw ServeLockHeldError if it already exists.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Layer boundary leak / contract inconsistency in plan-context payload trimming
description: Payload-budget byte-trimming is implemented in the MCP tool registration/presentation layer instead of the core service. The selection_token is minted in planContext() over the FULL candidate set before trimming runs, so the token's ai_selectable_stable_ids is a superset of the returned candidates — the token references candidates that were stripped from the response.
fix_direction: Move payload-budget trimming into the planContext() service layer before the selection token is minted, passing warnings/limits down into the service.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Fragile subcommand short-circuit in fabric config parent command
description: fabric config short-circuits subcommands (dismiss-slot, onboard-reset) by strictly checking process.argv[3]. If invoked with a flag before the subcommand (fabric config --target ./project dismiss-slot), argv[3] is '--target' not the subcommand, so the short-circuit is bypassed and the parent run launches the interactive Clack panel (or logs an uninitialized-workspace error and exits) instead of letting the subcommand finish.
fix_direction: Check whether the subcommand name exists anywhere in process.argv (process.argv.includes) or query the parsed citty command context instead of hardcoding argv indexes.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Unhandled promise rejection in experimental HTTP server /events endpoint
description: The /events endpoint passes the async handler from createEventsHandler directly. When ensureWatcher fails (server started in a non-Fabric dir, resolveLedgerPaths throws), the returned promise rejects. The router does not wrap this async call in a catch, so the rejection is unhandled — the connection hangs indefinitely or Express crashes without sending HTTP 500/diagnostic.
fix_direction: Wrap the /events handler in try/catch or an Express async wrapper to send a standard HTTP 500 via sendUnknownError when watcher setup fails.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Color-only and missing ARIA signaling for active registry tree node selection
description: The active tree item (dashboard.md) is styled with a unique color (purple dark / blue light) to signify selection but has no aria-selected/aria-current. Visually-impaired or color-blind users cannot tell which file is currently selected.
fix_direction: Add aria-selected=true (or aria-current=true) to the active node and manage it via JS on click/navigation.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: scrubRemoteUrl misses bare userinfo tokens (no ':' separator) -> credential leak
description: scrubRemoteUrl's regex expects a ':' password separator. Remote URLs with a bare userinfo token and no password (https://ghp_TokenSecret@github.com/org/repo.git) fail to match, so the token is not scrubbed and is persisted in plaintext into fabric-global.json or project fabric.json.
fix_direction: Change the scrubRemoteUrl regex to strip any credential/token in the userinfo segment even without a ':' separator.

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Experimental HTTP APIs expose unredacted ledger/history with personal-layer leakage
description: The experimental HTTP APIs (/api/ledger, /api/history/state, /api/replay) fetch and return the raw event ledger and historical state in plaintext with no content-based secrets/PII redaction, and read the full project ledger without filtering personal-scope (KP-*) events — violating cross-store privacy at the HTTP layer.
fix_direction: Add a visibility/scope filter to the ledger/history endpoints excluding personal-scope records, and apply redactSecrets before serializing payloads.

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: O(N^2) array linear scanning in recall ID path-filtering & intersection
description: Combined recall filters effective IDs against candidate IDs via effectiveIds.filter(id => candidateIds.includes(id)), then dedupes preserving order with a loop calling requestedIds.includes(id). Both arrays can grow to the retrieval limit N (or larger if a client passes a huge ID array), so .includes() inside .filter()/loop is O(N^2), costly at hundreds of IDs.
fix_direction: Convert candidateIds and requestedIds to Sets for O(1) .has() lookups, reducing to O(N).

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Unbounded HTTP session growth (no cleanup / idle expiration)
description: The experimental HTTP server holds all active SSE sessions in a module-level Map. onsessionclosed only deletes on clean SSE teardown; half-open connections, network drops, or repeated init requests accumulate sessions indefinitely. With no reaping/TTL/idle timeout the session map grows unbounded, a slow memory leak and eventual OOM under high concurrent traffic.
fix_direction: Add session eviction: a background reaper for sessions idle > TTL (e.g. 5 min), or cap max concurrent sessions.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Unnormalized multiline free-form fields in markdown persistence
description: user_messages_summary and session_context are written directly into markdown rule bodies with no line normalization or heading sanitization. Multiline user_messages_summary malforms the ## Evidence list; a field containing markdown headers (## Heading) corrupts structural section parsing in knowledge-meta-builder.ts.
fix_direction: Sanitize/escape leading '#' in free-form user text to prevent heading collision, and indent continuation lines of multiline summary values when embedding inside list bullets.

context.notes: Discovered by security perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Redundant BM25 IDF log calculations per scored document
description: In buildBm25Model, the returned scoreDoc recomputes IDF of query terms on every invocation via idf(term) (Math.log). IDF depends only on static corpus stats, so re-computing Math.log per term per scored doc wastes CPU.
fix_direction: Pre-compute/cache IDF per term when building the model, or memoize idf inside scoreDoc.

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Expensive synchronous recursive walk of all global stores in doctor checks
description: storeDoctorChecks loops all global stores calling findStoreExecutableViolations, a recursive synchronous directory scan (readdirSync/statSync checking stat.mode of every file), blocking the main thread with thousands of sync I/O ops for large KBs.
fix_direction: Make the walk asynchronous or cache scan results to avoid repeated synchronous I/O.

context.notes: Discovered by performance perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Explicit retrieval IDs silently truncated by semantic top-k budget
description: In combined recall, an explicit ids list is intersected with planContext semantic candidates via effectiveIds.filter(id => candidateIds.includes(id)). Because planContext truncates candidates to top_k (~30-50), any explicitly requested ID ranking poorly semantically is excluded silently, and the caller gets an empty result with no error/diagnostic that their requested rules were truncated.
fix_direction: Let explicitly requested ids bypass planContext top-k truncation, or make planContext aware of explicit ids and prioritize them so they are never omitted by the cap.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Non-atomic store scaffolding and race condition in initStore
description: initStore checks existsSync(identityFile) then mkdirSync + writeFileSync(identityFile,...) — not atomic. Interruption/disk-full mid-way leaves a half-initialized corrupted directory. With no lock-based concurrency control, concurrent storeCreate processes can both pass the existence check and overwrite store.json simultaneously.
fix_direction: Wrap initialization in withFileLock (or a local lock file) for mutual exclusion and roll back created folders on write errors to avoid partial-write corruption.

context.notes: Discovered by reliability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Code duplication: fab_recall bypasses the appendPayloadWarning helper
description: appendPayloadWarning is the unified standard for MCP tool payload warnings under v2.2 MC5. Peer tools (fab_get_knowledge_sections, fab_extract_knowledge, fab_review) call the shared helper, but fab_recall manually constructs the structured warning dict inline, risking warning-shape divergence.
fix_direction: Replace the manual inline warning assembly in recall.ts with the shared appendPayloadWarning helper imported from payload-warning.js.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Identically duplicated DEFAULT_IGNORES in HTTP server and CLI scanner
description: The DEFAULT_IGNORES patterns are identically duplicated between server-http-experimental/src/api/scan.ts and cli/src/scanner/ignores.ts, risking inconsistent scan results if one copy changes without the other.
fix_direction: Extract DEFAULT_IGNORES into @fenglimg/fabric-shared (or reuse cli/src/scanner/ignores.ts) and import it in scan.ts.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Inconsistent argument types (named vs positional) across store subcommands
description: fabric store add / create require alias and uuid as named flags (--alias foo --uuid 123), while remove/explain/bind/switch-write take them positionally (fabric store remove foo). This inconsistency forces developers to memorize arbitrary syntax differences for closely-related subcommands.
fix_direction: Standardize argument types across all store subcommands — make add/create accept alias (and uuid/remote) positionally or via unified flags matching remove/explain.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Silent exit code 0 when detaching/explaining non-existent store aliases
description: fabric store remove <alias> / explain <alias> on a non-existent alias prints an error but still exits with status 0, masking failures so shell scripts / CI cannot detect the command failed.
fix_direction: Set process.exitCode = 1 when the operation returns null (detached === null / explanation === null) before completing.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: fabric store switch-write lacks mounting validation, allows invalid targets
description: fabric store switch-write <alias> sets the project's active write store directly without verifying the alias is registered/mounted globally, so typos or unmounted aliases are written to project config silently, only failing later during write-heavy agent operations.
fix_direction: Verify the alias is mounted in global config (storeList()/storeExplain()) before writing it to project config; otherwise warn or throw a descriptive error.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Hardcoded English strings in fabric status bypass localization
description: Unlike other commands using getProjectTranslator(), fabric status hardcodes all labels/fallbacks in English ('uid:', '(no global config)', ...), breaking translation consistency for zh-CN users.
fix_direction: Use getProjectTranslator(process.cwd()) and add translation keys for status labels and fallback strings.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Missing accessible state for theme toggle button
description: The theme toggle uses classList.toggle('dark') but does not communicate state to screen readers — static aria-label='Toggle theme', no aria-pressed/aria-live. AT cannot know which theme is applied.
fix_direction: Add a dynamically-updated aria-pressed=true/false, or dynamically update aria-label to state the current/target theme.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: fabric sync prints raw untranslated store.state with no status markers
description: fabric sync output prints the raw store.state string in English (pending, synced, conflict, offline, aborted) without localization or colored status markers — a localization/readability barrier for non-English (zh-CN) users and no semantic cue for color-blind/low-literacy users.
fix_direction: Map store.state to localizable t(...) strings and paint with theme colors (paint.success for synced, paint.error for conflict/aborted, paint.warn for offline).

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Store lifecycle and config mutations bypass the event-ledger audit trail
description: File-level knowledge mutations are logged to events.jsonl, but store-level mutations — storeCreate, storeAdd, storeRemove, storeBind, storeSwitchWrite — never call appendEventLedgerEvent, leaving major topology and access changes completely unaudited.
fix_direction: Define store-lifecycle audit event schemas (store_mounted/store_created/store_detached/...) in event-ledger.ts and call appendEventLedgerEvent in the store-ops.ts orchestrations.

context.notes: Discovered by compliance perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Hardcoded candidate/sample limits silently truncate forensic analysis
description: The forensic scanner hardcodes SAMPLE_LIMIT=5 and CANDIDATE_FILE_LIMIT=12, silently truncating analysis. In large projects with many entry points or multi-framework topologies, only the first 5 entry points are analyzed and at most 12 candidate files output, causing incomplete framework recommendations and missed files at scale.
fix_direction: Move these limits to user-configurable / project config (fabric-config.json) or scale dynamically with project topology size.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Silent SSE ring-buffer truncation causes replay out-of-sync
description: The SSE events handler uses a fixed RING_BUFFER_CAPACITY=50 for replay history. If a client misses more than 50 events (disconnect or a burst like bulk save/re-index), older events are silently evicted; on reconnect with lastEventId the buffer cannot replay the oldest missed events, leaving the client out-of-sync with no warning.
fix_direction: Make ring-buffer capacity configurable/dynamic, or detect a lastEventId already evicted and return an explicit out_of_sync status prompting a full reload.

context.notes: Discovered by scalability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Dead code: unused export ResolverNotImplementedError
description: ResolverNotImplementedError is defined/exported in project-root-resolver.ts as a remnant of earlier P0.5 TDD stages; it is no longer thrown or imported anywhere, cluttering the public API surface of @fenglimg/fabric-shared.
fix_direction: Remove the ResolverNotImplementedError class definition and export.

context.notes: Discovered by maintainability perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Redundant watch paths in experimental HTTP server cache watcher
description: In createFabricHttpApp, chokidar watches both '.fabric/knowledge/**/*.md' and '.fabric/knowledge/pending/**/*.md'. The first glob already covers pending recursively, so the second is redundant and creates extra FS watch descriptors.
fix_direction: Remove '.fabric/knowledge/pending/**/*.md' from the chokidar watch array since it is already covered by the broader glob.

context.notes: Discovered by ux perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Redundant visual text and aria-label repetition (nav links, line 86)
description: Sidebar nav links define an aria-label identical to the link text (aria-label='Readiness' with text 'Readiness'), making screen readers read the name twice.
fix_direction: Remove the redundant aria-label when the visible text is already descriptive and identical.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)

title: Hardcoded English strings in onboard-coverage terminal output
description: The onboard-coverage human-readable renderer uses hardcoded English labels/columns ('Onboard coverage:', 'slot', 'status', 'entries', ...) despite translation support, so CJK users see an untranslated English block.
fix_direction: Wrap column headers, status strings, and helper text in renderHumanReadable with t(...) translator lookups.

context.notes: Discovered by accessibility perspective in DBP-20260531-103639 (engine=agy, round 2, new-ground steering; dedup deferred per user)
